body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: partial

## Primitive

The scenario exercises `streaming_output` — the agent emits assistant content across multiple partial flushes before a final turn-end signal, and the irrlicht invariant is that those partial flushes must NOT trigger a premature `→ ready` (no `ready → working → ready → working` flicker). The canonical signal is `ready → working → ready` with state pinned at `working` across every flush.

## agent_supports: yes

Aider supports streaming output. `replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` records `streaming_output: true`, and aider's docs confirm `--stream` (streaming pretty LLM responses) is enabled by default and only auto-disabled for models that lack support. In aider's transcript shape, the relevant 'partial flush' unit is per-model-call: under `--yes-always`, a single `####` user turn drives multiple model calls (e.g. an initial 'please add these files' reply, then auto-accepted file-adds, then a follow-up analysis), each delimited by a `> Tokens: …` line. The committed regression transcript `replaydata/agents/aider/regression/yes-always-multi-step/transcript.md` shows exactly this: two prose blocks (lines 12–20 and 30–34), each terminated by its own `> Tokens:` line within one `####` turn.

## irrlicht_observes: partial

The no-flicker invariant holds for the normal multi-flush case, but the end-of-turn detection has a documented idle-flush hazard, so the honest verdict is `partial`.

What works: the aider adapter is `FilesUnderCWD` + `RawLineParser` over `.aider.chat.history.md` (`agent.go:45-51`). The parser buffers plain prose lines silently (`parser.go:137-143`, returns nil) and emits `assistant_message` — NOT `turn_done` — at each `> Tokens:` line (`closeModelCall`, `parser.go:153-188`), keeping `turnOpen` true across model calls (verified by `TestParser_MultiModelCall_KeepsTurnOpen`). Critically, `IsAgentDone()` (`session.go:461-496`) treats only `turn_done` (and the Claude-specific `assistant`/`assistant_output` fallback) as done — aider's `assistant_message` is NOT matched, so per-call flushes cannot flip the session to `ready`. The session therefore stays `working` across all intermediate flushes, which is the invariant the scenario pins.

The gap: aider's only clean end-of-turn signal is `IdleFlush`, which synthesizes `turn_done` after the transcript has been quiet for `aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter` = 1500ms (`parser.go:54`, `216-229`). Because the markdown history is written as completed message blocks (terminal streaming is for the TUI; the file is not appended token-by-token — confirmed against aider's `--stream` / `--chat-history-file` docs), a slow remote model call that leaves the file quiet for >1500ms mid-turn (between flushes) can make IdleFlush synthesize a premature `turn_done`, producing a premature `→ ready` and thus a flicker. This is the carve-out that holds the verdict at `partial` rather than `yes`.

## Why not `yes`

The matrix-noted failure mode (premature ready via idle-flush during slow remote calls) is a concrete, source-grounded carve-out against the scenario's core invariant — not a narrow side detail — so per the authoring rules it pins `partial`, not `yes`-with-caveat.

## Why not `no`

The daemon DOES keep the session `working` across genuine back-to-back flushes (the common case), so observability is real, just not airtight. `no` would misrepresent the matrix.

## Matrix

Current coverage row already reads `agent_supports: yes`, `irrlicht_observes: partial`, note 'idle-flush may fire during slow remote model calls causing premature ready'. This assessment ratifies that verdict; no drift.
caveats: metric-drift-downstream: aider's end-of-turn is synthesized by IdleFlush after 1500ms of transcript quiet (parser.go:54); a slow remote model call that goes quiet >1500ms BETWEEN flushes can fire a premature turn_done → premature ready, flickering the no-flicker invariant the scenario pins.

feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: the scenario's canonical text describes Claude/JSONL assistant_streaming chunks (parser.go:288, non-terminal stop_reason); aider has no equivalent JSONL streaming event — its 'partial flush' unit is the per-model-call prose block terminated by a `> Tokens:` line, so the recipe must drive multi-model-call prose within one turn, not token-level streaming.

sources.note: streaming_output: true — confirms the primitive-exercised capability key

closeModelCall emits assistant_message (not turn_done) per `> Tokens:` line; turnOpen stays true; IdleFlush (aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter=1500ms) is the only clean end-of-turn signal (lines 54, 153-188, 216-229)
IsAgentDone (461-496): only turn_done (or Claude's assistant/assistant_output fallback) is 'done'; aider's assistant_message is NOT matched, so per-call flushes keep the session working
TestParser_MultiModelCall_KeepsTurnOpen + TestParser_IdleFlush_AfterMultipleModelCalls_EmitsTurnDone: turn stays open across multiple `> Tokens:` lines, closes only on idle flush
real transcript: two prose blocks within one #### turn, each terminated by its own `> Tokens:` line — the aider analog of partial flushes
--stream enabled by default (TUI streaming); --chat-history-file defaults to .aider.chat.history.md — file is written as completed message blocks, not token-by-token
aider streaming behaviour and chat-history markdown logging
